text:
Chapter 2

Cell: The Building Block of Life

Think It Over

Life

Where does a cell
come from?

It is widely accepted amongst the scientific community that life
originated in water. Some researchers believe that life may have
originated in small water pools with changing environmental
conditions rather than in the oceans. Hot springs are examples
of such environments. In India, the hot springs of Puga Valley in
Ladakh maintain very high springs of Puga Valley in
point of water even in a cold climate. These environmental
conditions seem to be similar to those on the early Earth, about
3.5 billion years ago. The organisms living in these hot springs
are mostly heat-loving bacteria called thermophiles, which are
unicellular.

Scientists from

diagrams.label: Cell Structure Diagram
diagrams.description: A large, circular diagram is presented, illustrating the internal structure of a cell. The diagram shows a boundary representing the cell membrane. Inside this boundary, various organelles and components are depicted in different colors. These internal structures include a central, large area (likely the nucleus) and several smaller, distinct organelles scattered throughout the cytoplasm. The overall representation is a detailed biological cross-section of a cell.
